maker: Modal Labs · CEO Erik Bernhardsson (ex-Spotify, author of luigi + annoy)
tagline: Serverless cloud for Python + ML.
paragraphs: What it is. Serverless cloud for Python + ML. Decorate a function with `@app.function(gpu="A100")` and it runs on a remote GPU on demand. Great for custom model serving + batch inference.

What sets it apart. Cold starts under a second even for GPU containers — famously hard problem solved elegantly. Python-native developer experience. Popular for one-off ML jobs + custom model endpoints.
Connections. Spotify heritage via Bernhardsson. Popular with research teams and indie ML developers.

meta.v: Tens of thousands of developers; beloved in Python ML circles
Custom model endpoints + Python batch jobs

name: Baseten
tagline: Managed model serving for custom / fine-tuned models.
paragraphs: What it is. Managed model serving for custom / fine-tuned models. "Truss" is their open-source packaging format. Focus: reliable production deployments with good observability.
What sets it apart. Strong story for enterprises with their own trained models who want managed infra without building it. Sits between Replicate (hobbyist) and the hyperscalers.
Connections. Used by Writer, Descript, Patreon for ML serving.

meta.v: Mid-market and enterprise ML teams
Custom-model production serving with hand-holding

name: Groq
maker: Groq · founded 2016 by Jonathan Ross (ex-Google TPU)
tagline: Inference on Groq's custom LPU (Language Processing Unit) chips.
paragraphs: What it is. Inference on Groq's custom LPU (Language Processing Unit) chips. Delivers the fastest tokens-per-second for open-weights LLMs available anywhere.
What sets it apart. Hundreds of tokens/second on Llama 70B-class models — demos feel instant, like reading a file. Founder invented the Google TPU.
Connections. Often confused with Elon Musk's xAI Grok. Has a Saudi sovereign deal for regional deployment.

meta.v: Popular for real-time voice agents + interactive UIs
Lowest-latency inference in the industry

name: Cerebras
tagline: Custom wafer-scale chip (a single chip the size of a dinner plate).
paragraphs: What it is. Custom wafer-scale chip (a single chip the size of a dinner plate). Runs inference on open-weights LLMs at speeds competitive with Groq.
What sets it apart. Only company shipping wafer-scale compute. Used by Glean, Meta, and the US national labs for high-throughput inference + training. Filed for IPO in 2024.
Connections. Rivalry with Groq for "fastest inference" mindshare. Different tech bet (wafer-scale vs. custom SRAM LPU).

meta.v: Larger enterprise contracts than Groq on average; smaller hobbyist mindshare
Enterprise-scale fast inference + training

name: RunPod
tagline: Raw GPU cloud — rent H100s, A100s, L40s by the hour for training, fine-tuning, or custom inference.
paragraphs: What it is. Raw GPU cloud — rent H100s, A100s, L40s by the hour for training, fine-tuning, or custom inference. Also offers serverless GPU mode.
What sets it apart. Cheapest GPU hour prices of any "real" cloud. Two-tier: "Community Cloud" (cheaper, less reliable) vs "Secure Cloud" (enterprise SLA). Huge with indie ML researchers.
Connections. Alternative to Vast.ai, Lambda Labs, Paperspace.

meta.v: Massive indie + research adoption; the "cheap GPU" default
Raw GPU rental / training on a budget

name: Pinecone
tagline: The original managed vector DB.
paragraphs: What it is. The original managed vector DB. Fully hosted, serverless mode, enterprise-grade. Launched the category in 2019 and defined the API most alternatives copied.

What sets it apart. Serverless pricing (pay per query, not per pod) makes it the easiest managed option for getting started. Largest enterprise customer base. Edo Liberty co-wrote the original vector-search research at Yahoo / AWS SageMaker.
Connections. Named in nearly every LangChain / LlamaIndex tutorial. Raised $100M Series B 2023.

meta.v: Most-adopted managed vector DB
Managed, serverless, enterprise-ready

name: Weaviate
tagline: Open-source vector DB with native multimodal support, built-in vectorization modules (you pass text, Weaviate embeds it), and hybrid BM25+vector search.
paragraphs: What it is. Open-source vector DB with native multimodal support, built-in vectorization modules (you pass text, Weaviate embeds it), and hybrid BM25+vector search.
What sets it apart. Weaviate Cloud (hosted) + self-hostable in a container. Hybrid search out of the box. Strong European customer base.
Connections. Ecosystem partner with Cohere, OpenAI, Hugging Face for embedding models. Named in most RAG tutorials alongside Pinecone.

meta.v: ~11K GitHub stars; popular OSS choice
Multimodal + hybrid search + self-host option

name: Qdrant
tagline: Rust-written vector DB with a focus on filtering + hybrid search.
paragraphs: What it is. Rust-written vector DB with a focus on filtering + hybrid search. Self-hostable single binary or Qdrant Cloud.

What sets it apart. Fastest pure performance benchmarks in the category, thanks to Rust. Good payload-filtering (i.e. "vector search but only in documents written after 2024"). Free tier has no row cap.
Connections. Used by OpenAI (internal), GitHub Copilot, X (Grok memory), and many LLM apps via LangChain.

meta.v: ~22K GitHub stars; strong growth 2024–2025
Performance + filtering

name: Chroma
maker: Chroma · founded by Jeff Huber & Anton Troynikov
tagline: Lightweight Python-native vector DB.
paragraphs: What it is. Lightweight Python-native vector DB. `pip install chromadb` and go. Designed for prototyping, notebooks, and small production workloads. Chroma Cloud adds managed hosting.
What sets it apart. Easiest onboarding of any vector DB — you can have a working RAG demo in 10 lines. Favorite for local dev + quick prototypes.
Connections. Default vector DB in most LangChain "getting started" tutorials. YC backed.

meta.v: ~17K GitHub stars; most-used for tutorials + prototypes
Simplicity, Python-native

name: Milvus (+ Zilliz Cloud)
tagline: Distributed, cloud-native vector DB designed for billion-scale collections.
paragraphs: What it is. Distributed, cloud-native vector DB designed for billion-scale collections. Open source; Zilliz Cloud is the managed version.
What sets it apart. Scale. Where Pinecone and Chroma handle millions of vectors, Milvus is built for billions — search engines, multimedia catalogs, recommendation systems.
Connections. Part of the LF AI & Data foundation. Powers vector search at Walmart, Nvidia, and others.

meta.v: ~30K GitHub stars; largest OSS vector DB by deployment count
Billion-vector scale + distributed architecture

name: pgvector (Postgres)
tagline: Postgres extension that adds vector types and HNSW / IVFFlat indexing.
paragraphs: What it is. Postgres extension that adds vector types and HNSW / IVFFlat indexing. Turn any Postgres database into a vector DB with one `CREATE EXTENSION`.

What sets it apart. Reuses your existing Postgres — same backup, same auth, same ops, same transactions. The "don't need another database" pitch killed many dedicated vector-DB pitches in 2024–2025. Supabase and Neon ship it by default.
Connections. Default vector store in Supabase, Neon, Tembo, AWS RDS Postgres, and most managed-Postgres offerings.

meta.v: Most-deployed vector storage globally (by virtue of Postgres ubiquity)
No new database, transactional, familiar SQL

name: Turbopuffer
tagline: Serverless vector + full-text search built on S3-style object storage.
paragraphs: What it is. Serverless vector + full-text search built on S3-style object storage. Optimizes for cost at "many namespaces × few queries each" — the common pattern for per-user AI apps.
What sets it apart. Orders of magnitude cheaper than Pinecone when you have millions of small tenants. Used by Notion, Cursor, Linear for AI features. Darling of the AI-infra twitter crowd.
Connections. Used by Cursor for codebase indexing across all its users. Founder was a Shopify principal engineer.

meta.v: Small but high-profile customer list; fast growth 2024–2025
Per-user / multi-tenant vector search at low cost
